exercises.title: Who wins the lock?
exercises.scenario: Only one instance can acquire a distributed lock.
exercises.expected_solution:
One of these three scenarios (exactly one true):
  a=true,  b=false, c=false  (Instance A wins)
  a=false, b=true,  c=false  (Instance B wins)
  a=false, b=false, c=true   (Instance C wins)

Never: a=true AND b=true (impossible)

Why: Redis is single-threaded for command execution.
  Even simultaneous network requests are queued and processed one at a time.
  The first SETNX: key doesn't exist → SET succeeds → returns 1 (true)
  Subsequent SETNXs: key exists → NX condition fails → returns 0 (false/null)
  Spring converts: 1→true, 0→null (which Boolean.TRUE.equals() treats as false)

Redis command: SET lock:job uuid-A NX EX 300
  NX = only set if key Not eXists
  EX = set expiry in seconds (atomic with SET)

test_cases.expected:
One of these three scenarios (exactly one true):
  a=true,  b=false, c=false  (Instance A wins)
  a=false, b=true,  c=false  (Instance B wins)
  a=false, b=false, c=true   (Instance C wins)

Never: a=true AND b=true (impossible)

Why: Redis is single-threaded for command execution.
  Even simultaneous network requests are queued and processed one at a time.
  The first SETNX: key doesn't exist → SET succeeds → returns 1 (true)
  Subsequent SETNXs: key exists → NX condition fails → returns 0 (false/null)
  Spring converts: 1→true, 0→null (which Boolean.TRUE.equals() treats as false)

Redis command: SET lock:job uuid-A NX EX 300
  NX = only set if key Not eXists
  EX = set expiry in seconds (atomic with SET)

hints: Redis processes commands one at a time — no true parallelism
SETNX = SET if Not eXists; second caller always finds the key already set
Spring's setIfAbsent returns null (not false) when key already exists

exercises.title: Complete the lock acquire and release
exercises.scenario: Minimal distributed lock pattern with UUID ownership.

blanks.hint: SETNX + EX atomic operation in Spring
Our UUID — compare to current value

hints: setIfAbsent with Duration: one atomic operation (SET NX EX)
Boolean.TRUE.equals(): null-safe true check (setIfAbsent can return null)
Owner check before delete: prevents deleting another instance's lock

exercises.title: Basic RedisDistributedLock service
exercises.scenario: General-purpose distributed lock with acquire, release, and runOnce.
exercises.instructions:
Write RedisDistributedLock @Service:
1. tryAcquire(key, ttl): returns UUID string if acquired, null if not
2. release(key, lockValue): delete only if value matches
3. runOnce(key, ttl, Runnable): acquire → run → finally release
4. Show the Redis commands generated by each method
hints: tryAcquire returns the UUID so the caller can verify ownership on release
release returns boolean: true if released, false if lock was already gone
runOnce try-finally: task exception doesn't prevent lock release

exercises.title: @Scheduled task with distributed lock guard
exercises.scenario: Prevent @Scheduled job from running on multiple instances simultaneously.
exercises.instructions:
Add distributed lock to Swiggy's daily deals refresh job:
1. Job runs every day at 2am
2. Lock key includes current date
3. TTL: 30 minutes
4. Log: which instance ran, which instances skipped
5. What happens if instance crashes mid-job?
hints: LocalDate.now().toString(): produces 'YYYY-MM-DD', unique per day
instanceId in logs: visibility for debugging which instance ran
TTL auto-expiry on crash: prevents permanent deadlock

exercises.title: Idempotency key for payment deduplication
exercises.scenario: Client retries on network timeout — idempotency key prevents double charge.
exercises.instructions:
Write PaymentIdempotencyService:
1. processPayment(idempotencyKey, request): check cache → acquire lock → process → cache result
2. Cache result in 'idem:result:{key}' for 24 hours
3. Processing lock 'idem:lock:{key}': 30-second TTL
4. If in-flight (lock exists): throw PaymentInProgressException
5. Show the happy path and the retry path
hints: Result key and lock key are different: result persists 24h; lock is ephemeral
Double-check: another thread may have finished while waiting for lock
24-hour TTL: covers typical client retry window (network timeout + retry)

exercises.title: Lock with retry — wait for lock to be available
exercises.scenario: Sometimes you want to WAIT for a lock instead of immediately giving up.
exercises.instructions:
Write LockWithRetry.tryAcquireWithWait(key, ttl, maxWaitMs):
1. Tries to acquire lock
2. If not available: wait 50ms and retry
3. Repeat until maxWaitMs is exhausted
4. Returns lock value if acquired, throws LockTimeoutException if not
5. When to use waitForLock vs tryAcquire? Explain
hints: Deadline-based retry: check System.currentTimeMillis() < deadline
Thread.sleep(50): short polling interval (not too tight, not too slow)
Thread.currentThread().interrupt(): restore interrupt flag after catching InterruptedException

exercises.title: @DataRedisTest: verify lock mutual exclusion
exercises.scenario: Test that exactly one out of two concurrent lock attempts succeeds.
exercises.instructions:
Write a test that:
1. Creates two threads both trying to acquire 'lock:test'
2. Verifies exactly one gets 'true' (acquired)
3. Verifies the other gets null/false
4. Verifies Redis key exists after one acquires
5. Verifies Redis key is gone after release
hints: CountDownLatch: wait for both threads to finish before asserting
AtomicInteger: thread-safe counter across concurrent threads
@AfterEach cleanup: always delete test keys so tests don't interfere

exercises.title: Cluster-safe @Scheduled batch report job
exercises.scenario: Monthly report generation — must run exactly once per month across the cluster.
exercises.instructions:
Write MonthlyReportJob for Razorpay:
1. @Scheduled: runs on 1st of each month at midnight
2. Lock key includes year-month: 'lock:report:2024-06'
3. TTL: 2 hours (report generation can take 30-60 min)
4. Logs: acquiring, generating, complete/skipped
5. Test: verify lock key has correct format and 2-hour TTL
hints: YearMonth.now().toString(): '2024-06' — unique per calendar month
Cron 0 0 0 1 * *: second=0, min=0, hour=0, day=1, any month, any DOW
TTL = 2× expected duration: generous buffer for slow months

exercises.title: Idempotency key store with result caching
exercises.scenario: Full idempotency service: lock + process + cache result + return cached on retry.
exercises.instructions:
Build IdempotentOrderService for Swiggy:
1. confirmOrder(idempotencyKey, orderId): process exactly once
2. Check 'idem:result:{key}' — return immediately if exists
3. Lock 'idem:lock:{key}' for 30 seconds
4. Process: save order confirmation, send to restaurant
5. Store result with 6-hour TTL
6. @SpringBootTest test: call twice with same key, verify method runs once
hints: AtomicInteger confirmationCount: tracks actual processing for test assertion
6-hour TTL for result: covers typical payment callback retry window
Double-check in lock: prevents race between two simultaneous first requests

exercises.title: What happens with no lock TTL?
exercises.scenario: Missing TTL creates a permanent lock on instance crash.
exercises.expected_solution:
1. TTL: -1 (no expiry — key lives forever until manually deleted)
   Redis CLI: TTL lock:job → -1

2. After instance crashes:
   The finally block never executes.
   redis.delete() is never called.
   'lock:job' remains in Redis with no TTL → PERMANENT.

3. Other instances:
   All calls to setIfAbsent('lock:job', ...) return false.
   No other instance can run the job.
   The system is PERMANENTLY LOCKED until someone manually deletes the key.

4. Duration affected:
   INDEFINITELY — until a human logs into Redis and runs: DEL lock:job
   This is a production incident requiring manual intervention.

5. Fix:
   Use the 3-argument setIfAbsent with TTL:
   redis.opsForValue().setIfAbsent("lock:job", "uuid-A", Duration.ofMinutes(15));
   Now: if instance crashes, TTL auto-expires after 15 minutes.
   Other instances can re-acquire after TTL.

test_cases.expected:
1. TTL: -1 (no expiry — key lives forever until manually deleted)
   Redis CLI: TTL lock:job → -1

2. After instance crashes:
   The finally block never executes.
   redis.delete() is never called.
   'lock:job' remains in Redis with no TTL → PERMANENT.

3. Other instances:
   All calls to setIfAbsent('lock:job', ...) return false.
   No other instance can run the job.
   The system is PERMANENTLY LOCKED until someone manually deletes the key.

4. Duration affected:
   INDEFINITELY — until a human logs into Redis and runs: DEL lock:job
   This is a production incident requiring manual intervention.

5. Fix:
   Use the 3-argument setIfAbsent with TTL:
   redis.opsForValue().setIfAbsent("lock:job", "uuid-A", Duration.ofMinutes(15));
   Now: if instance crashes, TTL auto-expires after 15 minutes.
   Other instances can re-acquire after TTL.

hints: TTL -1: key has no expiry in Redis
No finally on crash: JVM crash prevents cleanup code from running
3-argument setIfAbsent: generates SET NX EX (atomic lock + expiry)

exercises.title: Lock-based leader election
exercises.scenario: In a cluster, one instance acts as 'leader' for coordination tasks.
exercises.instructions:
Write LeaderElectionService:
1. tryBecomeLeader(): attempt to acquire 'lock:leader' with 30-second TTL
2. renewLeadership(): if we're the leader, refresh TTL before it expires
3. isLeader(): check if current instance holds the lock
4. stepDown(): release leadership voluntarily
5. @Scheduled: renew every 20 seconds (before 30s TTL expires)
hints: Renewal at 20s, TTL at 30s: 10-second buffer for network latency
Check instanceId equals current before renewing: guards against race on re-election
volatile currentLockValue: shared between @Scheduled and request threads

exercises.title: Razorpay payment dedup — full idempotency flow
exercises.scenario: Complete idempotency key implementation for payment processing.
exercises.instructions:
Build Razorpay's IdempotentPaymentProcessor:
1. process(idempotencyKey, merchantId, amount): full idempotency
2. 3 states: COMPLETED (return cached), IN_PROGRESS (409), NOT_STARTED (process)
3. On success: cache result JSON for 24 hours
4. On failure: delete lock but DON'T cache (allow retry)
5. Test: same key twice → method runs once, second returns cached
hints: Don't cache failure: on exception, only delete lock — next retry gets a clean attempt
Three states: check result first (fast), then check lock (slow), then process
finally: delete lock regardless of success or failure

exercises.title: Zerodha settlement with distributed lock + verification
exercises.scenario: Full end-of-day settlement with lock, crash-safe TTL, and verification test.
exercises.instructions:
Build ZerodhaSettlementService:
1. runSettlement(date): acquire lock, settle, release
2. Lock key: 'lock:settlement:{date}', TTL: 15 minutes
3. Persist 'settlement:done:{date}' = 'true' after completion
4. isDone(date): checks if settlement ran for that date
5. @SpringBootTest test (2 tests): verify runs once, verify isDone after run

All tests pass.
hints: Completion marker separate from lock: lock releases after job, but 'done' persists for 7 days
@BeforeEach cleanup: delete both lock and done keys to reset state
runCount.set(0) in @BeforeEach: reset counter between tests

exercises.title: Lua script for atomic lock release
exercises.scenario: True atomic compare-and-delete requires a Lua script.
exercises.instructions:
Write AtomicLockReleaseService using Redis Lua script:
1. Lua script: if GET key == value then DEL key return 1 else return 0 end
2. execute via RedisTemplate.execute(RedisScript, keys, args)
3. Wrap in a service with tryAcquire + atomicRelease
4. Explain why Lua is more correct than get-compare-delete
5. Test: verify atomicRelease returns false when value doesn't match
hints: DefaultRedisScript: wraps Lua script with return type
KEYS[1] and ARGV[1]: Redis convention for script parameters
redis.execute(script, keys, args): sends Lua to Redis eval

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay cluster coordination — synthesis
exercises.scenario: All distributed lock patterns combined: @Scheduled dedup, idempotency, leader election.
exercises.instructions:
Build RazorpayClusterCoordinator:
1. RedisDLock @Service: acquire (UUID), atomicRelease (Lua), runOnce
2. DailyReconciliationJob @Scheduled: runs at 2am, lock 'lock:reconcile:{date}', TTL=2h
3. IdempotentChargeProcessor.charge(key, amount): 3-state idempotency
4. LeaderOnlyNotifier: only leader sends system-wide notifications
5. Test (2 tests): reconciliation runs once, charge idempotent

All compiles and tests pass.
hints: Lua atomic release: prevents deleting another instance's lock after TTL expiry
@BeforeEach: clean up BOTH lock AND result keys for clean test state
runForDate helper: testable entry point (bypasses @Scheduled timing)
